Japanese: ‥‥１５年ぶり、だな。
English: It's been, what, 15 years?

Japanese: そうなりますね。
English: About that, yes.

Japanese: キミには 想像もつかないだろう‥‥。
English: 15 years is a long time to wait...

Japanese: この１５年間、私がどれだけ 苦しんだか‥‥。
English: You can't imagine how much I've suffered...

Japanese: あなたが‥‥苦しんだ？
English: You... suffered?

Japanese: そして、今。‥‥やっと、 チャンスがめぐってきた。
English: And now... the perfect opportunity presents itself.

Japanese: そう‥‥。 キミに復讐するチャンスがね。
English: At last... I shall have my revenge!

Japanese: なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: ‥‥メリー・クリスマス。
English: ...Merry Christmas.

Japanese: １２月２５日　午前１０時８分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: December 25, 10:08 AM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん。
English: Hey, hey, Nick!

Japanese: このあたりに、滝ないかな、 滝？
English: Do you know if there's any good waterfalls around here?

Japanese: タキ‥‥？ どうするんだよ、そんなの？
English: Waterfalls...? Dare I ask why?

Japanese: ナニ言ってるの、なるほどくん。
English: Duh, Nick! Isn't it obvious?

Japanese: 滝って言ったら、打たれるに 決まってるでしょ？
English: I need a waterfall to stand under! Preferably a freezing one!

Japanese: そういえば真宵ちゃんは “修行中の身”なんだっけ。
English: Oh... Is that part of your spirit medium training?

Japanese: そうだよ。‥‥最近、修行の方が おろそかになってるから、
English: Of course! Except, I've been slacking off lately...

Japanese: 滝ぐらい打たれておきたいな、 って思って‥‥。
English: I need to brave the elements and be forged anew under the rushing spring waters!

Japanese: うーん‥‥。
English: Umm... Okay...

Japanese: 近くに“ひょうたん湖”ってのが あるけど、滝はないなあ‥‥。
English: I don't know about any falls per se, but Gourd Lake is pretty close...

Japanese: うううう‥‥。
English: Oh. Darn.

Japanese: ないものはしょうがないだろ。 シャワーでも浴びといたら？
English: Sorry, but them's the breaks. Couldn't you just take a cold shower or something?

Japanese: うん、そうする。
English: Good idea!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (So much for the rushing spring waters...)

Japanese: ‥‥では、次のニュースです。
English: Next in the news...

Japanese: ひょうたん湖で、ナゾの巨大生物が 目撃されました。
English: A large, unidentified animal was sighted at Gourd Lake!

Japanese: ネス湖の“ネッシー”にちなんで “ヒョッシー”と名づけられ、
English: The town is buzzing with excitement!

Japanese: 大きな話題を呼んでいます。
English: Locals are calling it "Gourdy" in a tip of the hat to Nessie, the Loch Ness monster.

Japanese: 本家のネッシーがニセモノだった だけに、
English: Though its namesake, Nessie, proved to be a hoax...

Japanese: ヒョッシーは本当にいるのか？ ‥‥日本中の注目を集めています。
English: locals are confident their Gourdy is the real deal.

Japanese: あーあ‥‥ヒマだなあ。
English: *yawn* Bor-ing.

Japanese: ニュースも、ロクなこと やってないし‥‥。
English: Can't they show real news for a change?

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick?

Japanese: パワー、弱いよ。シャワー。
English: The water pressure's kind of low in that shower.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、そうだなあ。
English: You want more pressure, huh?

Japanese: 消防署に行って、ホースで 思いっきりぶっかけてもらったら？
English: Why don't you go down to the fire department and have them spray you with the hose?

Japanese: うん、そうする。
English: Good idea, Nick!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Apparently, E.S.P. is no aid in detecting sarcasm...)

Japanese: り‥‥臨時ニュースが入りました！
English: We interrupt this program to bring you a special news bulletin!

Japanese: ひょうたん湖で、今度は、 さ‥‥殺人事件です！
English: Strange occurrences continue at Gourd Lake... But this time, it's murder!

Japanese: （また、ひょうたん湖か‥‥）
English: (Gourd Lake again?)

Japanese: 今朝がた、ひょうたん湖から男性の 死体が見つかった事件で、
English: The body of a man was found in the lake early this morning.

Japanese: 警視庁は、逮捕した容疑者の 氏名を発表しました。
English: A suspect was apprehended. Sources inside the police department revealed...

Japanese: 容疑者の名前は、 ミツルギ・レイジ、２４才。
English: that the suspect's name is Miles Edgeworth, age 24.

Japanese: ミツルギは、将来を約束された、 有名な若手検事でした。
English: Edgeworth was an up-and-coming prosecution attorney, known for his skill and connections.

Japanese: その彼が、なぜ殺人を？ ‥‥日本中の注目を集めています。
English: He was guaranteed a long and rewarding career... has he thrown it all away?

Japanese: （ミ‥‥ミツルギ‥‥？）
English: (E-Edgeworth!?)

Japanese: な、な‥‥なんだってェ！
English: What's going on!?

Japanese: どういうことだ！ あのミツルギが‥‥？
English: Edgeworth would never do something like--

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick?

Japanese: わっ！　‥‥ま、真宵ちゃん！
English: Yipes! M-Maya!

Japanese: 電話したら怒られたよ。 ‥‥消防士さんに。
English: The fireman yelled at me when I called him.

Japanese: それどころじゃないよ！
English: We've got bigger things to worry about than that!

Japanese: 御剣が‥‥逮捕された！
English: They arrested Edgeworth!

Japanese: えっ！　‥‥あの、 ケンジさん？
English: What? You mean, the prosecutor?

Japanese: しかも、殺人容疑だぞ！
English: Yeah, he's a suspect... in a murder!

Japanese: えええええええっ！
English: Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: いつ？　どこで？　誰を？ どうして？　どうやって？
English: When? Where? Whom? Why? How?

Japanese: し、知らないよ！
English: I-I don't know!

Japanese: すぐ行こう！　なるほどくん！
English: Let's go find out, Nick!

Japanese: 千尋さんが“チャーリー”と 名づけてかわいがっていた植物。
English: Mia's favorite plant. Its name is "Charley."

Japanese: 最近は、真宵ちゃんも 世話に慣れてきたようで、
English: Maya's gotten the knack of watering it lately.

Japanese: チャーリーくん、元気を 取りもどしたみたいだ。
English: Charley's been perking up these days.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ちょっと読んでみたら、 アタマが痛くなった。
English: I tried reading one, and it made my head hurt.

Japanese: しまうとき、落っことして 足まで痛くなった。
English: When I closed it, it slipped out of my hand. Then my foot hurt too.

Japanese: 今日も向かいのホテルは 大はんじょうだ。
English: Looks like it's cleaning day again at the hotel across the way.

Japanese: なんでも今度、支店を 出すそうだ。
English: I hear they're planning a second branch outside the city.

Japanese: ボーイが、引き出しのドライバーの 角度をなおしているのが見える。
English: I can see the bellboy, getting the angle of that screwdriver in the drawer just right.

Japanese: 千尋さんが使っていたデスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: お客がもっと増えれば 座る機会もあるだろうが、
English: If we had more clients, I would probably sit here more often.

Japanese: 今はソファでテレビを 見ていることの方が多い。
English: Lately, I've been spending more time on the couch, watching TV.

Japanese: 一度、真宵ちゃんがトノサマンの ポスターを持ってきて、
English: Maya brought in a poster of the Steel Samurai the other day.

Japanese: 張る、張らないで大ゲンカに なってしまった。
English: We had a big fight over whether to put it up or not.

Japanese: まだあきらめきれないらしく、 スキをうかがっているみたい。
English: I know she's just waiting for a chance to sneak it up on the wall.

Japanese: どうしようか、これから。
English: Well, what should we do?

Japanese: ど、どうするって‥‥。
English: What do you mean!?

Japanese: とにかく、いろいろ 調べてみようよ！
English: Let's investigate!

Japanese: 御剣検事の話を聞いたり、 現場に行ったり‥‥。
English: We should talk to Mr. Edgeworth, and check out the crime scene!

Japanese: （そうだな‥‥最初から 迷っていても、しかたないか）
English: (You're right... We need more information! Save aimlessness and confusion for later!)

Japanese: ‥‥どう？ 何か気づいたこと、ある？
English: Well? Got any good ideas?

Japanese: うーん‥‥。
English: Not really.

Japanese: 滝に打たれたら、いい考えも 浮かぶんだけど‥‥。
English: I do my best thinking when I'm standing underneath a waterfall.

Japanese: どう？　最近、いいツヤが 出てきたと思わない？
English: You know, I think it's developing a nice luster.

Japanese: あとでゆっくり見てあげるから、 早く出かけようよー！
English: I'll look at your badge later, Nick. We've got work to do!

Japanese: （そりゃそうだな‥‥）
English: (I suppose you're right...)

Japanese: もう！　早くしないと、 御剣検事、死刑になっちゃうよ！
English: Nick! If we don't hurry Mr. Edgeworth will get the death sentence!!!

Japanese: わ、わかった。 ‥‥調査に行こうか。
English: Whoa! O-okay! Let's go check things out.

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: What is it?

Japanese: え。
English: Oh.

Japanese: いやあ。‥‥なんかちょっと、 引っかかることがねー‥‥。
English: Nothing... just, something's been bothering me.

Japanese: さっきの解剖記録、 ‥‥もう一度、見せてくれる？
English: Could you show me that autopsy report once more?

Japanese: あっ！ おも、思い出したよ！
English: Hey! I-I remember now!

Japanese: この人！
English: This guy!

Japanese: お姉ちゃんがいた事務所に いた、弁護士さん‥‥！
English: This is a lawyer that was at that office Mia worked at!

Japanese: 事務所に遊びに行ったときに、 会ったことがあるよ。
English: I met him once when I went there to hang out with Sis!

Japanese: 千尋さんがいた事務所‥‥？
English: That office...?

Japanese: それって‥‥、
English: Wait!

Japanese: ホシカゲ大先生の‥‥？
English: You mean Grossberg's office?

Japanese: そうそう！　その人！
English: Right! That guy!

Japanese: （‥‥まさかここで、あのセンセイ の名前が出てくるとは‥‥）
English: (That was the last name I expected to come up...)

Japanese: （ひさしぶりに、話を聞きに 行ってみるか‥‥）
English: (Maybe I should go talk to him... for old time's sake.)

Japanese: どうしようか？ これから‥‥。
English: Well, what should we do?

Japanese: そうだねー。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ケイサツは、もう御剣検事を 犯人だと決めつけてるし‥‥、
English: The police have pretty much made up their minds that Mr. Edgeworth did it.

Japanese: 御剣検事も、 何も話してくれないし。
English: And Mr. Edgeworth won't tell us anything.

Japanese: ‥‥公園で手がかりを 探すしかないよね。
English: I guess we could go look for clues down by the lake?

Japanese: そうだなあ。
English: Right.

Japanese: どう？ ‥‥何か気づいたこと、ある？
English: Well? Penny for your thoughts?

Japanese: うーん。
English: Thoughts... Thoughts...

Japanese: どうして御剣検事、何も 話してくれないんだろ。
English: Yeah, why won't Mr. Edgeworth tell us anything?

Japanese: それに。
English: And... and...

Japanese: “キミにはゼッタイたのまない” なんて。
English: And why did he refuse to ask for our help?

Japanese: ヒドいよねー。
English: What a jerk!

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick...

Japanese: その人、ホシカゲ先生の 事務所にいた弁護士さんだよ。
English: This person was a lawyer at Grossberg's office.

Japanese: 行ってみようよ‥‥事務所。
English: Let's go talk to him! Mr. Grossberg, I mean, not the dead guy.

Japanese: あ、そのカメラ‥‥ ナツミさん、だっけ？
English: Who was that with the camera...? Lotta something?

Japanese: そろそろ、写真のこと、 聞きに行ってみようよ！
English: Let's go talk to her about that photograph!

Japanese: あたしの‥‥おかあさん。 ‥‥‥‥。
English: My mother...

Japanese: （写真を見せたはいいが、 かけるコトバが見つからない）
English: (Now that I've shown her the picture, I can't think of anything to say...)

Japanese: （‥‥悪いことしちゃったな）
English: (That probably wasn't the best thing to do.)

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: December 25 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: 考えてみたら、
English: You know, Nick...

Japanese: あたしたち、みーんな ここに入ったことがあるんだね。
English: We've all been in here one time or another, haven't we?

Japanese: まあ、このシゴトをしていれば、 フツーじゃないかな。
English: I guess it comes with the territory.

Japanese: ‥‥誰かが聞いたら ゴカイするよ‥‥。
English: I'm not sure it's something we should mention to too many people...

Japanese: わっ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: こら！　帰るな御剣！
English: Hey! Edgeworth! Come back!

Japanese: ‥‥何しに来た！
English: What are you doing here!?

Japanese: なるほどくん、 フキゲンだよ‥‥御剣検事。
English: Nick, I don't think he's in a very good mood.

Japanese: そりゃ、 留置所に入れられて‥‥
English: Well, he is in detention.

Japanese: ゴキゲンって わけにもいかないだろ。
English: Were you in a good mood when you were here?

Japanese: こんな私を見て、笑いに来たのか！ ‥‥笑いたければ、笑うがいい。
English: So, you've come to laugh at the fallen attorney? Then laugh, laugh!

Japanese: どうした！　さあ、笑えよ！
English: Well? Why aren't you laughing?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ‥‥わ、笑った方がいいのかな？
English: Nick... Should we be laughing?

Japanese: こいつ、スナオじゃないからな。 笑ったら、怒り狂うか泣きだすぞ。
English: Nah. It's a trick. Laugh and he'll get mad... or burst into tears.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣。
English: Edgeworth.

Japanese: 笑いに来るほどヒマじゃないよ。
English: We don't have so much free time we can spend it coming down here to laugh at you.

Japanese: ヒマなくせに。
English: Yes you do.

Japanese: （まあ、ヒマはヒマだけど‥‥）
English: (Actually, he's right.)

Japanese: 見られたくなかった。
English: I hoped you wouldn't come. I didn't want you to see me.

Japanese: ‥‥こんなすがたを、キミにはな。
English: Not like this.

Japanese: （ぼくだって‥‥ 見たくなかったぞ‥‥）
English: (Hey, I didn't want to see you either, believe me.)

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: さっきから、まったく動かない。 さすが、プロだな。
English: He hasn't moved an inch since I came in. A real pro.

Japanese: 有名な検事、御剣を見て キンチョーしているのかも。
English: Or maybe he's just nervous with Edgeworth in the room.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: 御剣。 ‥‥事件のこと、聞かせてくれよ。
English: Edgeworth. Tell me what happened.

Japanese: そんなことを聞いて、 どうするつもりだ？
English: Why should I? What are you going to do about it?

Japanese: きまってるでしょ！ 助けるんですよ、御剣検事を！
English: Duh! We're going to help you, that's what!

Japanese: ‥‥助ける？　私を？　キミが？
English: Help me? You?

Japanese: うぬぼれるな。
English: Don't be ridiculous.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだ？
English: Sorry...?

Japanese: キミは、たかだか３回の法廷に 立っただけの、新米弁護士だ。
English: You're a novice! You've only been in three trials!

Japanese: なんだと？
English: H-hey!

Japanese: そりゃあ、運よく勝ったかも しれない。
English: Sure, you got lucky and won all three...

Japanese: だが、ラッキーは そう何度もつづかない。
English: But your luck's bound to run out some day!

Japanese: 実力だ。‥‥もっと 実力をつけたまえ。
English: You need real skill, Wright. Experience!

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick! He's insulting you! Nick?

Japanese: なんであたしたちが 怒られなくちゃいけないの！
English: Why am I always the one who has to get angry!?

Japanese: 事件は“ひょうたん湖”で 起こったんだろ？
English: The murder took place at Gourd Lake, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥昨日の深夜だ。
English: Yes... late last night.

Japanese: 湖って、裁判所や検事局とは ずいぶん離れてるよな。
English: The lake is a long way away from your offices and the court...

Japanese: 何をしに行ったんだ？ そんなところに？
English: Why were you down there?

Japanese: キミに言う必要はないだろう。
English: I see no need to tell you.

Japanese: み‥‥御剣検事‥‥。
English: M-Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: まさか‥‥ホントに、人を‥‥？
English: You... you didn't really...?

Japanese: ヒョッシー。
English: Gourdy.

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ヒョッシーを見に行ったのだ。
English: I went to see Gourdy.

Japanese: “ひょっしー”？
English: "Gourdy"?

Japanese: なあに、それ？
English: What's that!?

Japanese: あとで教えてあげるよ。
English: I'll... tell you later.

Japanese: （御剣のヤツ‥‥ぼくたちには 話せないのか‥‥？）
English: (Why won't Edgeworth talk to us?)

Japanese: 御剣‥‥。 こんなこと、聞きたくはないけど。
English: Edgeworth... this is really hard for me to ask...

Japanese: まさか‥‥きみがやったんじゃ ないだろうな？
English: But... you didn't do it, right? Right?

Japanese: ‥‥どう考えるのも キミの自由だ。
English: Think what you will.

Japanese: しかし。 １つだけ、たのみがある。
English: I have only one request.

Japanese: なんだ？
English: Huh?

Japanese: この事件に、 かかわらないでほしい。
English: Stay out of this case.

Japanese: どうしてですか！
English: Why!?

Japanese: なるほどくんは、 あなたを助けようと‥‥！
English: B-but Nick is trying to help you!

Japanese: わかっている！ ‥‥わかってるさ！
English: I know...! I know that!

Japanese: でも私は、きみたちには 助けてほしくないのだ！
English: But I don't want your help, okay?

Japanese: どうして‥‥？
English: Why not?

Japanese: とにかく！ 私のことは、ほっといてくれ！
English: Look, just go away, and leave me alone!

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Nick...

Japanese: 御剣さん、やっぱり‥‥ 犯人なのかな
English: Mr. Edgeworth did it, didn't he.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！ ‥‥他のところを調べよう。
English: Maya! Let's go investigate elsewhere.

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: But, Nick...

Japanese: 弁護士バッジ‥‥か。
English: Your attorney's badge...?

Japanese: 御剣。
English: Edgeworth.

Japanese: ぼくにやらせてくれないか、 きみの弁護を。
English: Let me defend you.

Japanese: ハ‥‥、ハッ！
English: Hah! Hah hah!

Japanese: ワルい冗談はよしてくれ。
English: Good one, Wright.

Japanese: 私はそこまで 落ちぶれてはいないつもりだ。
English: But I'm not that hard up. Not yet.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Wh-what do you mean by that?

Japanese: たまたま３回、勝訴しただけの 新米にすがるなんて、ゴメンだ。
English: Me? Trust a wet-behind-the- ears lawyer with only three trials under his belt? Never!

Japanese: なんですって！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: たしかに私は、非常に不利だ。
English: My case is near hopeless, Wright.

Japanese: 私が依頼した弁護士たちは、 すべて断ってきた。
English: Every defense attorney I've talked to has turned me down.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだ？
English: What?

Japanese: “私には、自信がありません” ときたもんだよ。
English: Simply put, they were afraid they'd lose.

Japanese: ‥‥もしかしたら、
English: It occurred to me that it might be my fault that they lack confidence.

Japanese: 彼らの依頼人を、かたっぱしから 有罪にしてやったからかもしれん。
English: After all, I did get every single one of their clients declared "guilty."

Japanese: そんなわけ、ないだろう！
English: I don't believe it!

Japanese: とにかく、 私にはかまわないでほしい。
English: Regardless, I don't want you involved in this.

Japanese: きみには‥‥きみにだけは、 依頼するつもりはない！
English: You in particular I cannot ask to do this.

Japanese: なんだそれは？
English: What's that?

Japanese: 今、ムダ話をする気には なれない。
English: I'm not in the mood for idle banter, Wright.

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: December 25 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。
English: What's this?

Japanese: あれだけイヤミを言ったのに、 また来たのか‥‥。
English: I was hoping you'd gotten my message the first time.

Japanese: 御剣。弁護士はどうなった？
English: Edgeworth, what about your defense?

Japanese: キミにはカンケイないことだ。
English: It's no concern of yours.

Japanese: （どうやら、まだ 見つかってないらしいな）
English: (Guess he hasn't found anyone yet.)

Japanese: 成歩堂。
English: Wright.

Japanese: 今さらこんなことを たのむのも、あつかましいが、
English: It pains me to ask you this now...

Japanese: わかった！　“ベンゴ”ですね！
English: I know! You want us to defend you!

Japanese: ‥‥おねがいできるだろうか。
English: Yes... Will you?

Japanese: やっと、そのコトバが聞けたな。
English: Ah, who could have guessed this day would come?

Japanese: まさか、こんな日が来るとは 思わなかった。
English: Not me.

Japanese: 悪いが、ゴメンだね！
English: Sorry, charley! No way!

Japanese: 成歩堂‥‥。
English: Wright...

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Nick...!?

Japanese: い、いや、そんな シリアスにならないでほしいな。
English: H-hey, don't look so serious, guys!

Japanese: 冗談に決まってるだろ！
English: I was kidding!

Japanese: この場をなごませようと、 カルいジョークを‥‥
English: Just a little joke!

Japanese: カルくも、
English: There was nothing little...

Japanese: ジョークでもないよッ！
English: ...or joke-like about it!

Japanese: （けられてしまった‥‥）
English: (I thought it was funny...)

Japanese: ほらっ！ やりなおしなさい！
English: Nick! Do it over!

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥断るわけないだろ？　御剣。
English: How could I refuse you, Edgeworth?

Japanese: すまない。成歩堂。
English: Thank you, Wright.

Japanese: これでやっと、“借り”を 返せるな‥‥御剣。
English: This is my chance to finally pay you back.
Pay him back...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥？　なんのことだ？ おぼえがないぞ‥‥。
English: Pay me back?
For what? I don't remember ever doing anything for you.

Japanese: いいんだよ、それでも。
English: Never mind... I guess you don't really need to know.
Huh...

Japanese: 依頼状を書いた。 ‥‥刑事にわたしてくれ。
English: My letter of request. Please give it to Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: 《御剣　怜侍の依頼状》を ポケットにしまった。
English: Edgeworth's Request put in pocket.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、そろそろ‥‥。
English: Well, I guess we should...

Japanese: な、なんだ‥‥？
English: W-what's that!?

Japanese: じ‥‥地震だよ！ なるほどくん！
English: E-earthquake! Nick!

Japanese: お‥‥大きいよ‥‥！
English: It's a big one!

Japanese: わわわわわ‥‥。
English: Whaaaaa!

Japanese: ‥‥よ、弱まってきたね‥‥。
English: I-it's calming down...

Japanese: ううう‥‥ビックリした‥‥。
English: Whew... that was scary!

Japanese: あれ？　御剣は？
English: Huh? Where's Edgeworth?

Japanese: いた。
English: There.

Japanese: 床にうずくまって、 ふるえてるよ。
English: He's on the floor in a ball, shivering.

Japanese: キライなのかな、地震？
English: I guess he doesn't do so well with earthquakes.

Japanese: （そんな話、 聞いたことないけど‥‥）
English: (I've heard of running, but curling up in a ball?)

Japanese: まあ、いいか。
English: Well, I guess we're done.

Japanese: 検事さん、コシがぬけて 立てないみたいだし。
English: Mr. Edgeworth doesn't seem like he's going to stand up any time soon.

Japanese: ‥‥行こ、なるほどくん。
English: Let's go, Nick.

Japanese: ああ‥‥。
English: Uh... right.

Japanese: 御剣からもらった依頼状、 イトノコ刑事にわたさなきゃな。
English: We have to give Edgeworth's letter of request to Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: 御剣。‥‥事件のことだけど。
English: Can I ask you about the murder?

Japanese: 成歩堂。
English: Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥たのむから、私のことには かまわないでくれ。
English: I'll ask you again, just leave me alone.

Japanese: いいかげん、わかってほしい。
English: Please try to understand.

Japanese: 強がりや、キミを見下している ワケではないんだ。
English: I'm not doing this to prove I'm tough, or because I look down on you.

Japanese: キミには、この事件を調べて ほしくない。
English: I just don't want you anywhere near this case. Understand?

Japanese: なぜ、ひょうたん湖に 行ったんだ？
English: Why did you go to Gourd Lake?

Japanese: キミに教えるつもりはない。
English: I have no intention of telling you.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事にも 教えないつもりらしいな。
English: Nor, apparently, would you tell Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: シンパイしてましたよ、 イトノコさん‥‥。
English: Detective Gumshoe was really worried about you!

Japanese: “ＤＬ６号事件”‥‥ 父が亡くなった事件だ。
English: The "DL-6 Incident"... was when my father died.

Japanese: 私の目の前で、な‥‥。
English: Right before my eyes...

Japanese: 父は、私の目の前で撃たれたのだ。
English: He was shot and killed, and I saw it all.

Japanese: 実は、そのときの記憶は、 ハッキリしない。
English: My memories from that time are... foggy.

Japanese: 私のココロの中で、かたく 閉ざされている。
English: I suppose it's a self-defense mechanism.

Japanese: とにかく、容疑者として、 １人の男が逮捕された。
English: In any case, a suspect was arrested... a man.

Japanese: 状況から考えて、父を 撃てたのは、そいつだけだった。
English: It's pretty clear he was the only one who could have killed my father.

Japanese: 父の霊を呼び出した霊媒師も、 犯人はその男だと言っていた。
English: The spirit medium they used to talk to my late father said the same thing.

Japanese: そいつを弁護して、無罪にした のが、生倉弁護士だ。
English: It was an attorney by the name of Robert Hammond that cleared the suspect's name.

Japanese: 今回の事件の、被害者だな？
English: And Hammond... is the victim in the Gourd Lake murder?

Japanese: そうだ。
English: Correct.

Japanese: あの‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: その霊媒をしたの‥‥、 あたしの母、なんです‥‥。
English: That spirit medium... that was my mom.

Japanese: なんだって！　キミが‥‥？
English: What? You mean you're...?

Japanese: フシギなものだな。
English: It's strange.

Japanese: 事件が終わろうという 今になって、こんなことに‥‥。
English: I thought that terrible incident was about to end, and now... this.

Japanese: 事件が‥‥“終わる”？
English: "About to end"?

Japanese: “ＤＬ６号事件”は、１５年前に 起こった。
English: The DL-6 Incident happened 15 years ago.

Japanese: １５年前の、１２月２８日だ。
English: 15 years ago... on December 28.
December 28?

Japanese: ‥‥あと３日で、時効なのだよ。
English: The statute of limitations on the case runs out in three days.

Japanese: なんだって！
English: What!?

Japanese: なるほどくん？ ‥‥“時効”って？
English: Um, Nick? What does that mean?

Japanese: 時効が成立すると、もうその 事件は、なかったことになるんだ。
English: When a case's statute of limitations runs out, legally, the case never happened.
Three days from now, DL-6 will be closed... forever.

Japanese: その、ムザイになった“容疑者” って、どうなったんですか？
English: What happened to the suspect? The one who got off innocent?

Japanese: ‥‥わからない。
English: I don't know...

Japanese: 世間からすがたを消して、 そのあとのことは誰も知らない。
English: He disappeared from public view. Nobody knows where to.

Japanese: 生きていれば、今年で５０才 ぐらいだろうな‥‥。
English: If he's still alive, he'd be about 50 years old now.

Japanese: （それだけ大きな事件の 容疑者だったんだ‥‥）
English: (I guess I can understand why he'd go into hiding...)

Japanese: （きっと、まともな生活は 送れなかったんだろうな）
English: (It'd be hard to live a normal life after being a murder suspect in such a big case.)

Japanese: あの、あの、お父さんて、 弁護士さんだったんですよね？
English: Umm... so, was your father a lawyer?
He was...

Japanese: 御剣　信（みつるぎしん）。 当時は有名だったようだ。
English: Gregory Edgeworth. He was quite famous at the time, apparently.

Japanese: 御剣検事も、お父さんを めざしていた、とか‥‥。
English: So, you were sort of trying to follow in his footsteps.

Japanese: ムカシの話だ。
English: I'd rather not talk about it.

Japanese: “弁護士バッジ”‥‥か。
English: Your attorney's badge...?

Japanese: 今となっては、見たくない シロモノだな。
English: I can't say I really want to see one of those right now.

Japanese: すまない。 語るべきコトバが見つからない。
English: I'm sorry. I can't think of anything I want to say about that.

Japanese: 御剣？
English: Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥キミがおとずれてから、 たかだか数時間‥‥。
English: It's only been a matter of hours since you last visited.

Japanese: それなのにもう、そこまで たどりついてしまったのか‥‥。
English: Yet you've made incredible progress in your investigation.

Japanese: さすがだよ。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: I'll admit it, I'm impressed, Wright.

Japanese: ムカシからそうだ。
English: You were always single- minded in your work, though.

Japanese: キミは、一度決めたことは かならず、やりとげる男だったな。
English: Once you start on something, you always see it through, don't you?

Japanese: “ＤＬ６号事件”‥‥。
English: About the "DL-6 Incident"...

Japanese: そうだ‥‥“ＤＬ６号事件”。
English: Right... DL-6.

Japanese: この事件‥‥キミにだけは、 知られたくなかった。
English: I didn't want you to find out about it.

Japanese: だから、キミの弁護の申し出を ことわった。
English: That is why I refused your offer to defend me.

Japanese: キミをバカにするようなことを 言って、
English: I'm sorry if it sounded like I thought you weren't up to the job.

Japanese: この事件に近づかないように したつもりだったのに‥‥。
English: I just wanted to keep you away from DL-6.

Japanese: ‥‥メイワクだったか‥‥？
English: So... do you still think it would have been better for me to stay away?

Japanese: わからない。
English: I don't know.

Japanese: ‥‥だが。
English: But...

Japanese: ここまできて、キミにかくしごとを するのは、もう意味がない。
English: I see no point in hiding anything from you now.

Japanese: よかろう。
English: Very well.

Japanese: なんでも聞いてくれたまえ。 ‥‥答えさせてもらうよ。
English: Ask whatever you like, and I will answer to the best of my abilities.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: Hmph...

Japanese: こんな写真があったとはな。
English: Who would have thought there'd be a photo...?

Japanese: 御剣‥‥。 お前、撃ったのか？
English: Edgeworth... Did you shoot him?

Japanese: キミはどう思う？ 成歩堂。
English: What do you think, Wright?

Japanese: お前は、誰かに 銃を向けられる男じゃないよ。
English: I don't think you're the kind to point a gun at anyone, no.

Japanese: ‥‥撃ってませんよね？
English: So you didn't shoot him?

Japanese: ああ。 ‥‥私ではない。
English: No, I didn't. It wasn't me.

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖公園　入口前
English: December 25 Gourd Lake Park Entrance

Japanese: ここが‥‥事件のあった？
English: This is where it happened?

Japanese: そう。 ひょうたん湖のある公園だよ。
English: Yeah. Gourd Lake is in the middle of this park.

Japanese: 警官たちがウロついているね‥‥。
English: I can see some police walking around in there.

Japanese: 聞き込み、かな。
English: Questioning people, probably.

Japanese: あれ？　‥‥あそこにいるの、 イトノコ刑事‥‥だね。
English: Hey! Isn't that Detective Gumshoe over there?

Japanese: どうなんだッ！
English: Well, pal!?

Japanese: これだけいて、何かつかんだヤツは いないのか！
English: There's enough of us here! Anyone found anything?

Japanese: す‥‥すみません。 まだ‥‥。
English: S-sorry, sir... Nothing.

Japanese: バカモン！ 裁判は明日だぞ！
English: Idiot! The trial's tomorrow!

Japanese: ‥‥何としても、 手がかりを見つけるんだ！
English: We need clues, on the double!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥。
English: B-but, sir...

Japanese: こうして手がかりがないから、 御剣検事殿は、逮捕されて‥‥
English: There weren't any clues... that's why we arrested that attorney, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥そうですよ。 犯人は、検事殿‥‥
English: It's clear, sir. He's the one who--

Japanese: うるさいッ！
English: Shaddup!

Japanese: もう一度、 そんなことを言ってみろ！
English: Just you try saying that again!

Japanese: えーと、その‥‥ユルさんぞ！
English: I'll... er... I'll make you sorry if you do!

Japanese: 早く行けッ！
English: I mean... just, get outta my face, pal!

Japanese: は、はッ！
English: Y-yes, sir!

Japanese: イトノコさん‥‥コワい‥‥。
English: Detective Gumshoe's kinda scary today!

Japanese: まったく。
English: Recruits... peh!

Japanese: あッ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: きゃッ！
English: Eek!

Japanese: アンタはたしか、 ヤッパリくんだったッスな！
English: Hey, you're that Harry guy! Harry Butz!

Japanese: ナルホドです！ （いいかげん、おぼえろよ！）
English: Wright! Phoenix Wright! (Will he ever learn my name!?)

Japanese: 何しに来たッス！　調査ッスか！
English: And just what are you doing here, pal? Investigating!?

Japanese: え？　‥‥ええ、そうですが。
English: Huh? Um, well, yes. I suppose.

Japanese: 全面的に協力するッス！ なんでも聞くッス！
English: Well, I'm here to help! Ask me anything you want!

Japanese: さあ、カモンッス！
English: Bring it!

Japanese: ‥‥いつもと違うよ‥‥。 どうしちゃったんだろ？
English: He seems different than usual. I wonder what's up?

Japanese: あの‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: あたしたち、まだ御剣検事から 弁護を依頼されたワケじゃあ‥‥。
English: Mr. Edgeworth hasn't actually asked us to defend him yet...

Japanese: え！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: な、なんだ‥‥。 そうだったッスか‥‥。
English: Oh? Y-you don't say...

Japanese: イトノコさん！
English: Detective Gumshoe, sir!

Japanese: どうした！ 何か見つかったか！
English: What? Find something!?

Japanese: いえ。それはまだ‥‥。
English: Um, no, sir. Not yet.

Japanese: 署から連絡が入りました！ ‥‥捜査会議だそうです！
English: But there was a call from the precinct. They want to hold an investigation briefing...

Japanese: 捜査会議‥‥？
English: A briefing?

Japanese: わかった！ ‥‥すぐもどる！
English: Right! I'm off!

Japanese: あー。
English: Oh...

Japanese: ‥‥すまんッス。 聞いたとおりッス。
English: Sorry, pal. I guess you heard. I gotta go.

Japanese: 何か、聞いておくことは あるッスか？
English: Any last things you want to ask me about before I head back?

Japanese: あ、じゃあ、 今回の被害者のデータを‥‥。
English: Well, yes. Do you have any information on the victim...?

Japanese: ‥‥すまんッス。
English: Sorry...

Japanese: 死体の解剖記録は、まだ できてないッス。
English: They haven't worked up the autopsy report yet.

Japanese: 自分も報告を待っているッス。
English: I'm still waiting for it myself.

Japanese: ‥‥そッスね‥‥。
English: Actually...

Japanese: 時間があったら、署の方に 来てほしいッス！
English: Say, if you get the time, drop by the precinct!

Japanese: 話のつづきは、そこで するッス！
English: We can talk more there, pal!

Japanese: イトノコさん、もう もどって来ないんですか？
English: You're not coming back, Detective?

Japanese: うーん‥‥。 たぶん、ムリッスね。
English: Erm... Probably not, pal.

Japanese: じゃあ、お話ししたいことが できたら、どうすれば‥‥？
English: So, what should we do if we have something to talk to you about...?

Japanese: そッスね‥‥。
English: Ah, right.

Japanese: 署の場所を教えるから、 そっちの方へ来てくれるッスか？
English: Here, I'll show you how to get to the precinct. Come down and see me anytime.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事から、警察署の 場所を教えてもらった。
English: Detective Gumshoe gave you directions to the police station.

Japanese: あ！　ちょっと！ ‥‥イトノコ刑事！
English: Oh, hey! Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: な‥‥なんスか！
English: W-what!?

Japanese: あの‥‥公園の中、 調べたいんですけど‥‥、
English: Um, we'd like to take a look around in the park.

Japanese: 歩きまわってもいいですか？
English: Can we walk around?

Japanese: ああ！　いいッス！ キョカするッス！
English: Yeah! No problem, pal. You got my permission.

Japanese: いやあ、なるほどくん。
English: You know, Nick.

Japanese: キョカをもらうには、その人が 忙しいときをねらうにかぎるね！
English: I think there's something to be said for talking to people when they're busy.

Japanese: 生活のチエ、ってヤツだな。
English: Yeah. They don't have time to think about not giving you information...

Japanese: さあ！ ‥‥いろいろ調べてみようよ！
English: Right! Now, let's get investigating!

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。
English: Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、 何があったんですか？
English: Do you know what happened here?

Japanese: えっ？　知らないッス？ 事件のコト‥‥。
English: Huh? You don't know, pal?

Japanese: はあ。
English: No...

Japanese: まったく、あきれた オトボケ弁護士ぶりッスな‥‥。
English: Wow, okay, Mr. head-in-the- fluffy-pink-clouds Lawyer.

Japanese: オトボケ‥‥。
English: Head-in-the... huh?

Japanese: いッス。教えるッス。
English: Never mind, I'll tell you.

Japanese: 昨夜の、午前零時１５分ごろの ことッス。
English: It happened last night, about 15 minutes after midnight.

Japanese: ひょうたん湖に、ボートが１つ、 浮かんでいたッス。
English: There was a boat out on Gourd Lake.

Japanese: ボートには、２人の男が。
English: In that boat were two men.

Japanese: そして、片方の男が、 ピストルでもう一方を‥‥。
English: One of those men shot the other with a pistol.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、御剣検事 だったんですか？
English: And... the shooter was Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: かけつけた警官が 逮捕したッス‥‥。
English: A cop who arrived on the scene arrested him.

Japanese: ずいぶん、手回しが いいですね。
English: How did he get there so fast?

Japanese: ‥‥それッス。
English: Well...

Japanese: 目撃者がいたッス。
English: There was a witness.

Japanese: そいつの通報で、ワレワレは すぐにかけつけられたッス。
English: When the report came in, we raced to the lake.

Japanese: 目撃者‥‥。
English: A witness?

Japanese: 目撃者、というのは‥‥？
English: Who was this witness?

Japanese: ワルいッスが‥‥、 それは捜査のヒミツッス。
English: Er, sorry, pal. That's confidential.

Japanese: とにかく、その人物は、 すべて見ていたらしいッス。
English: Anyway, the witness saw everything, apparently.

Japanese: おそらく、明日の法廷に 出てくると思うッス。
English: I'm sure they'll turn up at the trial tomorrow.

Japanese: 目撃者は、 その１人だったんですか？
English: Was there only that one witness?

Japanese: ‥‥そうみたいッス。 昨日の夜は特にサムかったし、
English: Yep. It was pretty cold out on the lake last night.

Japanese: それにホレ、クリスマス・イブ だったッスから。
English: And, it was Christmas Eve after all.

Japanese: でも。
English: Still, we're being thorough.

Japanese: 他にも目撃者がいたかも しれないと思って、
English: You never know when you're going to turn up another witness.

Japanese: こうして聞き込みをしてるッス。
English: That's why we're here today, checking things out.

Japanese: 今のところ、成果は 上がっていないッスけど‥‥。
English: So far, we're coming up empty...

Japanese: そうか。 今日はクリスマスなんだ‥‥。
English: Oh! It's Christmas today! I'd forgotten.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 なんかちょうだい。
English: What are you getting me for Christmas, Nick?

Japanese: サンタさんにおねがいしなさい。
English: Talk to Santa.

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥ まさか、サツジンなんて‥‥？
English: You don't think Mr. Edgeworth is a... murderer!?

Japanese: あり得ないッス！ 自分は信じてるッス！
English: Absolutely not! It's impossible!

Japanese: たとえ目撃者がいたって、 そんなコト、カンケーないッス！
English: I don't care if there's a witness either! I don't believe a lick of it!

Japanese: そ、そうですよね！ カンケーないッスよね！
English: R-right! Who cares what the witness says!

Japanese: （カンケーないことあるか！）
English: (I care!)

Japanese: でも。
イトノコさん、御剣検事のコト、 信頼してるんですね。
English: You really believe in him, don't you, Detective?

Japanese: トーゼンッス！ ‥‥しかし‥‥、
English: Course I do!

Japanese: ‥‥正直なところ、警察ではもう、 御剣検事が犯人ということに‥‥。
English: But... the police are pretty sure he's the killer.

Japanese: この聞き込みも、みんな、あまり マジメにやってないッス。
English: Nobody's even really taking this investigation that seriously.

Japanese: そんな‥‥！
English: Oh no!

Japanese: みんな、あれだけ御剣検事の お世話になってきたのに‥‥。
English: After all the help Mr. Edgeworth has been to us...

Japanese: 今じゃあ、彼を信じようとする 者は、いやしないッス‥‥。
English: Hard to imagine that no one's standing up to take his side.

Japanese: イトノコさん‥‥。
English: Well, at least you are, Detective. At least you are.

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥、明日の裁判の 弁護は、どなたが‥‥？
English: Do you know who will be Mr. Edgeworth's defense attorney in tomorrow's trial?

Japanese: まだ、決まってないッス。
English: He hasn't got one yet.

Japanese: えっ！
English: What?

Japanese: ‥‥明日ですよね、裁判‥‥？
English: The trial IS tomorrow, isn't it?

Japanese: くわしいことは 教えてくれなかったッスが‥‥、
English: Well, I don't know the whole story...

Japanese: どうやら、引き受けてくれる 弁護士が、いないらしいッス。
English: But apparently no one he's been talking to will take his case.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか？
English: W-why not?

Japanese: 御剣検事は教えてくれないッス。
English: Mr. Edgeworth won't tell me!

Japanese: アンタたちが来たときは、 てっきり引き受けてくれたのかと。
English: When you guys showed up, I figured he'd asked you to defend him.

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: U-unfortunately not...

Japanese: おねがいッス！　彼の チカラになってほしいッス！
English: Well, pal, then you got a job to do! Help out Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: その胸のバッジが、 ただのカザリじゃないことを、
English: Prove that badge you wear isn't just some fancy piece of metal!

Japanese: 自分に証明してほしいッス！
English: Prove it to me, pal! Show me you're an attorney!

Japanese: あの、あの‥‥。 ホントなんですか？
English: I-is it true?

Japanese: 御剣検事の弁護、誰も 引き受けてくれない、って。
English: No one will take Mr. Edgeworth's case?

Japanese: ‥‥そッス。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: 御剣検事は、ちょっとした 有名人ッス。
English: He's a bit of a celebrity.

Japanese: その裁判で負けてしまっては、 自分たちの名前にキズがつくッス。
English: If you defended him, and lost, your reputation'd be sure to suffer.

Japanese: それに。
English: What's more...

Japanese: 御剣検事は、かなり不利ッス。
English: The case against him is... well, it's pretty solid.

Japanese: （目撃者がいるもんな‥‥）
English: (I suppose it would be if they have a witness.)

Japanese: アンタ！
English: Hey! Pal!

Japanese: アンタまで、 彼に背を向けるッスか！
English: Don't tell me you're going to turn your back on him too!?

Japanese: トノサマン事件を思い出すッス！
English: Remember the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: 御剣検事の協力があったから、 アンタの依頼人は無罪に‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth helped you get your client declared innocent!

Japanese: わかってますよ！
English: I... I know.

Japanese: でも‥‥。
English: I went to Edgeworth. I tried.

Japanese: 御剣は、ゼッタイに たのむ気はないそうですよ。
English: He really doesn't want us to represent him.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくにだけは、ね。
English: Especially not us, he said.

Japanese: なんスとぉぉぉ！
English: What!?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そんなハズはないッス！
English: W-well, that doesn't make any sense, pal!

Japanese: トノサマンの事件が終わったとき、 検事は言っていたッス！
English: You should have heard him talking about you after the Steel Samurai case!

Japanese: “さすが成歩堂だ”って。 ‥‥何度も言っていたッス！
English: He kept saying "Wright, Wright, Wright" over and over.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick?

Japanese: 何が“さすが”なのかなあ？
English: I'm not sure that's a good sign.

Japanese: ‥‥わかんない。
English: Neither am I...

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしてッスか‥‥ 御剣検事。
English: Why wouldn't he want your help?

Japanese: わ、わからないッス‥‥。
English: I don't get it.

Japanese: そのバッジ！
English: That badge!

Japanese: つきつける相手を 間違っているッス！
English: You're showing that to the wrong guy, pal!

Japanese: それを必要としてるヒトに 見せてあげてほしいッス！
English: You gotta show that to someone who needs it!

Japanese: 悪いッスが‥‥、
English: Er, sorry.

Japanese: それは事件にはカンケイ なさそうッスね。
English: I'm too busy with this investigation for small talk.

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖広場
English: December 25 Gourd Lake Public Beach

Japanese: うわあ。 ‥‥これが“ひょうたん湖”？
English: Wowsers! This is "Gourd Lake"?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: Yup.

Japanese: “うわあ”って言うほどの こともないけどね。
English: I'm not sure it warrants a "wowsers," though.

Japanese: まあ、たしかにね。
English: Hmm. Probably not.

Japanese: それより、あれ！ ‥‥あの屋台！
English: But hey, look at that snack stand!

Japanese: “とのさまんじう”って‥‥。
English: "Samurai Dogs"...?

Japanese: あーん、食べたいよー！ トノサマンジュウ！
English: I wanna Samurai Dog! Please!

Japanese: きっと、スゴクおいしいよ！
English: I bet they're great!

Japanese: たしかに、マンジュウだからなあ。
English: With a name like Samurai Dog, how could they not be?

Japanese: でも‥‥。なぜ今、あえて “トノサマン”なのかな？
English: They're a little behind the times, though.

Japanese: ヤングがムチュウなのは、 “ヒメサマン”なのに‥‥。
English: The kids are all into "The Pink Princess" now.

Japanese: ねえ。
English: I mean, like, y'know!?

Japanese: （‥‥“ねえ”と言われても‥‥）
English: (Nope.)

Japanese: あ。こんなところに クラッカー。
English: Huh. Someone left one of those poppers here. You know, you pull the string...

Japanese: クリスマスだからね。
English: ...and it goes "pop." Yeah, I know the ones. You see them a lot around New Year's.

Japanese: ね。ね。何かの手がかりに なるかもしれないよ。
English: Hey, Nick! It might be a clue!

Japanese: もらっておこうか。
English: Let's take it.

Japanese: 自分が鳴らしてみたい だけだろ？
English: C'mon, admit it. You just want to pop it, right?

Japanese: あは。バレちゃった？
English: Was it that obvious?

Japanese: （クラッカーか‥‥どうしよう？）
English: (A popper... hmm.)

Japanese: そうだね。じゃあ‥‥
English: I suppose it couldn't hurt.

Japanese: あれ？　クラッカーがない。
English: Huh? Where'd it go?

Japanese: もうポケットにしまっちゃったよ。
English: I already put it in my pocket.

Japanese: ポケットに《クラッカー》を そっとしまった。
English: Popper put in pocket.

Japanese: やめておこう。
English: Nah, let's leave it.

Japanese: ‥‥自分にスナオに生きた方が たのしいのに‥‥。
English: Aww, listen to your inner child a little, Nick!

Japanese: （なんか言ってるよ‥‥）
English: (Maybe if your outer child would be quiet for just a moment, Maya!)

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖広場
English: December 25 Gourd Lake Public Beach

Japanese: ケーサツの人、聞き込み あきらめたみたいだね‥‥。
English: Looks like the police have given up their questioning.

Japanese: おい！
English: Hey!

Japanese: わっ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: なるほどくん！　‥‥サンタさんに インネンつけられたよ！
English: N-Nick! I think Santa's mad at you!

Japanese: ひさしぶりだな、成歩堂。
English: Long time no see, Nick.

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥サンタさんに 知り合いがいるの‥‥？
English: Nick... you know Santa!? Wow... Nick and St. Nick... Hey! I see the connection!

Japanese: い、いないよ！
English: Don't be ridiculous!

Japanese: オレだよオレ！
English: Dude, it's me!

Japanese: や‥‥矢張！
English: L... Larry!

Japanese: 何やってんだ こんなところで！
English: What are you doing here!?

Japanese: 決まってんだろ！ バイトだよバイト。
English: Isn't it obvious? I'm working my day job!

Japanese: マンジュウ売ってるんだよ。
English: I sell Samurai Dogs! Want one?

Japanese: カズミとのデート、 カネがかかるからさぁ！
English: Gotta get money for dates, you know. My girl Kiyance deserves the best!

Japanese: （こ、こいつ‥‥ まだコリてないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (K-Kiyance...? Not another model, I hope...)

Japanese: カズミはいい女だぞぉ‥‥。
English: Oh, Kiyance's a fine, fine woman, Nick.

Japanese: “これ着た方がァ、売り上げェ？ ノビるんじゃないかなァ”って、
English: It was her idea that I wear this costume! She was all "You go girlfriend!" Y'know?

Japanese: この服、プレゼントして くれたんだよ！
English: She bought this costume for me!

Japanese: ‥‥とほほ。
English: That... that's great, Larry.

Japanese: へええ。いいヒトなんですねぇ！
English: Wow! A Santa costume! She must be really nice!

Japanese: おッ！　か、かわいい‥‥。
English: Whoa! Cute!

Japanese: 成歩堂！ この子、アンタのなんなのさ？
English: Nick! Who's she? She's not your...?

Japanese: な、なんなのさ、って‥‥。
English: Not my... what? N-no, she's not!

Japanese: あ、あの、あたし、 綾里　真宵です。
English: I'm his partner, Maya Fey.

Japanese: ええと、その。妹です。
English: I'm, uh, the little sister.

Japanese: ‥‥イモウト‥‥？
English: Sister...?

Japanese: そうか。 タイヘンなんだなあ、成歩堂‥‥。
English: Wow, Nick, must be tough.

Japanese: こんなトシゴロのイモウトを やしなって、弁護士を‥‥。
English: Working nine to five, having to take care of a little sister...

Japanese: いや、あの。 ‥‥お姉ちゃんの妹‥‥。
English: N-no, I'm not Nick's sister, I'm my older sister's little sister...

Japanese: ふううん。 ‥‥いい話よなあ。
English: Huh. Sounds great!

Japanese: （聞いてない）
English: (Don't worry, Maya, he's not listening...)

Japanese: なあ、矢張。
English: Hey, Larry.

Japanese: 昨日の夜、ここで殺人事件が あったんだけど‥‥、
English: There was a murder here last night...

Japanese: ここでアルバイトしてたんなら、 何か知らないか？
English: You work here. Have you heard anything?

Japanese: ムリだよ、なるほどくん。
English: Nick, you're wasting your time.

Japanese: 昨日の夜は、 クリスマス・イブだよ。
English: Last night was Christmas Eve!

Japanese: ヤッパリさん、コイビト いるんだよ。カズミさん。
English: He was with Kiyance, obviously!

Japanese: こんなサムいところに、 いるワケ、ないじゃない。
English: He wouldn't have been standing out here in the cold!

Japanese: う、ううッ‥‥！
English: Oof!

Japanese: 今のヒトコト、なんか はげしくキズついたみたいだぞ。
English: I think what you just said caught him off guard, Maya.

Japanese: い、いや‥‥カズミのヤツ、
English: N-no, it's just... Kiyance's not in town right now.

Japanese: 今、撮影でグアムに行っててさ。 ‥‥ちょっと、会えないワケよ。
English: She... she's in Hawaii on a photo shoot.

Japanese: （相手は、またモデルか‥‥）
English: (A model. I knew it.)

Japanese: この湖で、殺人があったんだよ。 明日、その裁判があるんだ。
English: Well, anyway, there was a murder here on the lake. The trial's tomorrow.

Japanese: はあん。
English: Huh. Neat.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人は、あの御剣　怜侍だ。
English: The defendant is Edgeworth. Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Um, Nick?

Japanese: ヤッパリさんが御剣検事のこと、 知ってるワケが‥‥
English: Why would Larry know Edgeworth...?

Japanese: 成歩堂！
English: Whoa, Nick!

Japanese: ミツルギって‥‥あの？
English: You don't mean THAT Miles Edgeworth!? Old Edgey!?

Japanese: そうだよ。‥‥あいつ、 殺人事件の容疑者なんだ。
English: Yeah. He's a murder suspect.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥。
English: Wh-whoa! Murder?

Japanese: え？　え？　ヤッパリさん、 御剣検事のことを‥‥？
English: Huh? You know Mr. Edgeworth, Larry?

Japanese: 知ってるよ！　モチロン！
English: Yeah! Of course!

Japanese: オレたちのクラスメートだからな！ ‥‥小学校のころの。
English: Edgey was in the same class as us in grade school!

Japanese: ええええええええええええええっ！
English: Whaaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: あの。あの。 おマンジュウのことですけど！
English: Umm... umm... tell me about the dogs!

Japanese: ん？　ああ。 トノサマンジュウのコト？
English: Huh? Oh, you mean the Samurai Dogs?

Japanese: なんでアレがトノサマンジュウ なんですか？
English: W-why are they Samurai Dogs?

Japanese: なんていうか、ひょうたんの形に 見えますけど‥‥。
English: I... I mean they kind of look gourd-shaped.

Japanese: ああ。アレ、もともとは “ひょうたん湖マンジュウ”だし。
English: Oh, well originally, they were Gourd Dogs! Y'know, like "guard dogs"?

Japanese: え。
English: Ouch...

Japanese: いや、カズミがサ。 ‥‥オレの女なんだけど。
English: The Samurai thing was Kiyance's idea. Oh, she's my woman, y'know.

Japanese: “名前変えた方がァ、売り上げェ？ ノビるんじゃないかなァ”って、
English: She was all "change the name and you go girlfriend!"

Japanese: ノボリを作ってくれたんだよ！
English: She made me that banner!

Japanese: おかげで、子供たちに 大人気なんだぜ！
English: Man, the kids can't get enough of those Samurai Dogs!

Japanese: うう‥‥。
English: Erm...

Japanese: なんか、やり場のない イキドオリを感じる‥‥。
English: Something about that just seems... wrong.

Japanese: それにさ。
English: Oh, and guess what?

Japanese: 最近は、例のニュースで 人出も多いんだよ、この湖。
English: We're getting a ton of customers here at the lake, what with the big news!

Japanese: “れいのにゅうす”‥‥？
English: The "big news"...?

Japanese: あれだよ、“ヒョッシー”。
English: Yeah! Gourdy!

Japanese: “ひょっしー”‥‥？
English: G-"Gourdy"...?

Japanese: あの‥‥なんですか？ “ヒョッシー”って？
English: Umm... what's "Gourdy"?

Japanese: なんだよ。知らねぇの？
English: Huh? You mean you don't know?

Japanese: この湖にさ。いるんだよ。 ‥‥巨大なカイブツが‥‥。
English: It's here, in this very lake! A giant, mysterious monster! Gourdy!

Japanese: カイブツ？
English: A... monster?

Japanese: ああ。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: これ、昨日の雑誌の記事だ。 ‥‥写真もあるぜ。
English: Check it out. This is an article from yesterday's newspaper. There's a photo!

Japanese: わっ！ ホ‥‥ホントだ！
English: Wow! It's r-really real!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ カイジュウだよカイジュウ！
English: Nick! A monster! A real monster!

Japanese: う‥‥うん。 （‥‥ホントにいたのか‥‥）
English: Umm... yeah. (It's probably just a log or something... right?)

Japanese: お。‥‥この写真を撮影した Ａさんの話。
English: Hey... there's a quote here from the person who took the photo.

Japanese: なになに。
English: Hmm? What's this?

Japanese: 《自動シャッターでカメラを セットして、２人で並んだとき、
English: "I set the camera to automatic, and when we got into the frame..."

Japanese: “パアン！”っていう大きな 破裂音がしたんです。
English: "I heard a loud 'bang'! Like an explosion..."

Japanese: そのあと、背中の方で かすかに水の音が聞こえました》
English: "followed by the sound of something slipping into the water..."

Japanese: あたしも見たいな‥‥。
English: I wish I could have seen it!

Japanese: （なんで“破裂音”なんだ‥‥？）
English: (Why would there be a sound like an explosion...?)

Japanese: 矢張。
English: Larry.

Japanese: よかったらこの記事、くれないか？
English: Could I borrow this article from you?

Japanese: ああ、いいぜ。 １００万円で売ってやるよ。
English: Sure, no problem. That'll be one million dollars!

Japanese: ひゃ、ひゃくまんえん‥‥。
English: O-one million...?

Japanese: （コドモかお前は‥‥）
English: (Grow up, Larry.)

Japanese: 《ヒョッシーの記事》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Gourdy Article added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥もう一度 その記事、見せて。
English: Hey Nick, show me that article once more.

Japanese: 《自動シャッターでカメラを セットして、２人で並んだとき、
English: "I set the camera to automatic, and when we got into the frame..."

Japanese: “パアン！”っていう大きな 破裂音がしたんです。
English: "I heard a loud 'bang'! Like an explosion..."

Japanese: そのあと、背中の方で かすかに水の音が聞こえました》
English: "followed by the sound of something slipping into the water..."

Japanese: ‥‥うーん、ロマンチックだねー。
English: How romantic!

Japanese: すごいなあ、カイジュウ。
English: And cool! I mean, to think, a real monster!

Japanese: な。‥‥マンジュウも売れて、 ありがてェかぎりさ。
English: Yeah. I'm just glad I'm selling more dogs!

Japanese: 御剣検事、ヤッパリさんの 同級生だったんですか！
English: So, Mr. Edgeworth was your classmate, Larry?

Japanese: そうさ。成歩堂と３人、 よくツルんでたぜ。
English: Yeah, Nick, him, and I used to hang out all the time.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったんだ‥‥。
English: Wow... I never knew.

Japanese: あいつ、あのころから おボッチャンでさ。
English: Don't get me wrong. He's always been kind of a stick in the mud.

Japanese: “おとーさんみたいになるんだ” って、おベンキョウしてたよ。
English: Studying all the time, trying to "be like father."

Japanese: “お父さんみたいに”‥‥？
English: Like his father...?

Japanese: ああ。アイツのオヤジ、 有名な弁護士だったんだよ。
English: Yeah. Edgey's pop was a famous defense lawyer back in the day.

Japanese: へええ。
English: Wow.
Wait...

Japanese: ‥‥“有名な弁護士”？
English: You said "defense lawyer"?

Japanese: そう。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: ちょっと待って！
English: Wait a second!

Japanese: 御剣さんは、“検事”なんですよ！
English: But Mr. Edgeworth is a prosecuting attorney!

Japanese: ダレだよ、ケンジって。
English: What? Edgey's got a proboscis on his knee!?

Japanese: ケンジじゃなくて検事！
English: No, no--he's a "prosecuting attorney."

Japanese: 弁護士の“逆”みたいなもの。
English: That's like the total opposite of a defense lawyer!

Japanese: へえ‥‥。 そりゃ、知らなかったなぁ。
English: Huh. Go figure!

Japanese: どうしちゃったんだ、アイツ？
English: He always used to talk about defending the "weak" who were "unable to defend themselves."

Japanese: あのころは、“ぼくは、コドクな ヒトの味方になるのだよ”なんて、
English: Man, he used to go on and on about man's duty to society and all that. What a bore!

Japanese: ハズかしくなるような理想を さんざんカタっていたのにな‥‥。
English: I wonder what changed his mind, though?

Japanese: なあ、成歩堂？
English: Do you know, Nick?

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥？
English: Nick...?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士バッジか。
English: Huh? Oh, that's your attorney's badge, isn't it?

Japanese: おマエにゃあ、 セワになったよなあ。
English: Dude, you really helped me out back in that trial.

Japanese: オレ、たいしたお礼も できないけど‥‥
English: S-sorry, I can't really pay you.

Japanese: （女の子につぎこむからだろ！）
English: (Cause you blow it all on "Kiyance"...!)

Japanese: よかったら、マンジュウ 好きなだけ食ってってくれ。
English: But... you can have all the dogs you want!

Japanese: い、いいんですか！
English: R-really!?

Japanese: ‥‥矢張。
English: Larry...

Japanese: この子に食べさせると、 今日はシゴトがなくなるぞ。
English: If you let her at your dogs, you won't have any left for the other customers.

Japanese: ‥‥いいよ。彼女がよろこんで くれるんなら、サ。
English: Hey, no problem. If that's what makes her happy, y'know?

Japanese: （‥‥何度も言うけど、 助けたのはこのぼくだぞ‥‥）
English: (How many times do I have to remind you that it was ME who got you off the hook!)

Japanese: 矢張、こいつ、知ってるか？
English: Hey Larry, you know this guy?

Japanese: 誰これ。
English: Who's this?

Japanese: 知らないから聞いてるんだよ。
English: I don't know. That's why I asked you.

Japanese: 誰これ？　真宵ちゃん。
English: Who's this, Maya?

Japanese: ううーん‥‥。
English: Well...

Japanese: （そういえば、真宵ちゃんは 見たことがあるって言ってたな）
English: (Oh, wait. Maya said she had seen him before, didn't she...)

Japanese: こ、これ、なんだよ？
English: W-what's this?

Japanese: オレ、目が悪いから よく見えないけど‥‥
English: You know, my eyes have been getting pretty bad lately, Nick...

Japanese: もともとそんな写真なんだよ。
English: Actually, the photo's blurry, Larry.

Japanese: 昨日の夜、撮った写真だ。
English: It was taken last night.

Japanese: ゆ、ゆうべか‥‥。
English: Last night...?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ、コレ。
English: Whazzat?

Japanese: カメラっていうんだ。 写真はふつう、コレで撮る。
English: It's a camera. You take pictures with it.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥。
English: Huh?

Japanese: や、ヤッパリさん！ ‥‥カメラ、知らないんですか！
English: L-Larry! You mean you don't know what a camera is!?

Japanese: し、知ってるよ！ ‥‥オレだって中学は出てるんだ！
English: C-course I know! Hey, you're looking at a bona fide junior high graduate!

Japanese: オレが聞いたのは、 カメラについてるヘンなキカイ！
English: I was talking about the weird contraption on that camera!

Japanese: ああ、それね。
English: Oh, that.

Japanese: ‥‥説明するのがメンドくさいから トボけてみたんだ。
English: Well... it's hard to explain. Just forget about it.

Japanese: じゃあ、最初から見せるな！
English: Well don't go showing me it, then! Geez!

Japanese: 悪いな、成歩堂。
English: Sorry, Nick.

Japanese: マンジュウ売ってるサンタにゃ、 なんのことかわかんねえよ。
English: I don't know much about that. I'm just a simple vendor of Samurai Dogs, dig?

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 貸しボート小屋前
English: December 25 Boat Rental Shop

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥ここは？
English: Nick... what is this place?

Japanese: “貸しボート”屋だよ。
English: A boat rental shop. Closed for Christmas it seems.

Japanese: ボートで殺人があっただけに、 今日はやっていないみたいだね。
English: I guess a murder taking place on one of the boats won't be good for business, either.

Japanese: ボートかあ。 ‥‥あたし、乗ったことないよ。
English: Boats... I've never ridden on a boat.

Japanese: 事件がカイケツしたら、 乗りに来ようか？
English: Really? Well, how about we go out on one when the trial is finished?

Japanese: お。いいね、それ。
English: Hey, good idea! You bet!

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 ひょうたん森
English: December 25 Gourd Lake Woods

Japanese: いいところだねぇ なるほどくん。
English: I like it here, Nick.

Japanese: 見て。‥‥誰か、 キャンプなんかしてるよ。
English: Look... someone's camping!

Japanese: 殺人事件があったってのに、 ノンキなもんだな。
English: They've got guts, camping at the scene of a murder.

Japanese: ねえねえ。
English: Hey, hey, Nick!

Japanese: キャンプしてたんなら、何か 事件のこと、知ってるかも‥‥。
English: If they were camping here last night, they might know something about the murder!

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに、 その可能性はあるか‥‥）
English: (That's true...)

Japanese: そうだね。‥‥ちょっと、 話を聞いてみようか。
English: Good call, Maya. Let's go talk to them.

Japanese: ああああああああああああああっ！
English: Yaaaaaaaaaaaaaaw!

Japanese: コラァッ！　さわったらアカン！
English: Hey, you! Git yer hands offa' that!

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Eek!

Japanese: あーあ‥‥。
English: What in the Sam Hill...!?

Japanese: どーしてくれるん！ フィルム１本、ムダになったやん！
English: Look what ya done now! There goes a whole roll of film!

Japanese: え。あ。その。ど、 どうも‥‥すいませ
English: Ah? Wha... huh? S-sorry!

Japanese: ムッチャ高いフィルムやねんで？ ゴメンですめばケーサツいらんわ！
English: Sorry's nice but it don't pay my bills! Y'all know how much a roll of that film costs!?

Japanese: あ、あたし、その。 ‥‥ベンショウしますから
English: I-I'll pay you back!

Japanese: なァんでこんなトコで クラッカー鳴らすかなァ！
English: What were ya'll thinkin' settin' off a party-popper in a place like this?

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Uh... well...

Japanese: なんや！
English: What!

Japanese: “あら、カンサイ人てケチですね” みたいなカオして！
English: Don't try to play stupid with me just 'cause you think I'm some country bumpkin!
Yeah, I know how y'all Yanks think!

Japanese: ゆうとくけど、 カンサイ人は、ケチちゃうで！
English: "I say, those southern folks talk with that exaggerated drawl, why they must be dumb!"

Japanese: ウチが個人的にケチなだけや！
English: Well let me tell you, just because I might be dumb don't mean we all are!

Japanese: なるほどくん、たすけてぇ
English: N-Nick, help!

Japanese: なんやアンタは！ このおじょうちゃんのホゴシャ？
English: And who are you, now? Her chaperone?

Japanese: え。‥‥いや、なんというか、 しりあい、みたいな
English: Yeah, uh, no, rather, uh... we're sort of... friends?

Japanese: はいはいはい、もっとチャキチャキ しゃべる！
English: Jus' figure out what y'all are gonna say and say it for bejeezum's sake!

Japanese: ほんッと、こっちの人間と しゃべると、つかれるわァ。
English: Gawd, I'd rather sit through one of papa's drawls than listen to you stutter all day.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Oh boy...)

Japanese: （とりあえず、お金は 払っておくか‥‥）
English: (I guess we should pay her for the film...)

Japanese: 気ィつけや！
English: Watch it!

Japanese: ハイ。
English: Yes ma'am.

Japanese: （あとで払えよ！）
English: (On second thought, I'll pay later...)

Japanese: ゴメンナサイ‥‥。
English: I'm really sorry!

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick.

Japanese: このカメラ、なんかスゴいよ‥‥。
English: Check out this camera!

Japanese: ホントだ。‥‥ヘンなキカイと マイクがついてる。
English: Yeah... what's with the big mic and that box on top, I wonder?

Japanese: どうやら、何かの音に反応して シャッターを切るみたいだな。
English: It looks like some sort of automatic system for taking a photo when a noise is heard.

Japanese: へえ！　すごいなあ。 ‥‥ちょっと、やってみようか。
English: Wow! Hey, let's see if it works!

Japanese: コホン。
English: *cough*

Japanese: “ぼく、なるほどくん！”
English: "Hi, I'm Nick!"

Japanese: 声が小さいのかな。
English: Maybe I'm not saying it loud enough.

Japanese: “なーるーほーどーくーん！！”
English: "YO! I'M NICK!!!"

Japanese: オカシイな‥‥。
English: Huh.

Japanese: なあーるうー ほおーどおー‥‥
English: NIIIIIIIIII......IIIIIIIIIICK!

Japanese: や、やめてくれ ハズかしい！
English: Will you stop that?

Japanese: コワれてるよ、このキカイ！
English: I think it's broken, Nick.

Japanese: けるな！
English: D-don't kick it!

Japanese: （反応する音が、人間の声じゃ ないんじゃないかな‥‥）
English: (It must not be set up to respond to voices.)

Japanese: このカメラ‥‥。 マイクとキカイがセットされてる。
English: This camera has a mic and some sort of attachment.

Japanese: たぶん、何かの音に反応して シャッターを切るんだろう。
English: It must take pictures when triggered by a noise.

Japanese: へえ！　すごいなあ。 ‥‥ちょっと、やってみようか。
English: Wow! Cool! Let's try it out!

Japanese: コホン。
English: *cough*

Japanese: “ぼく、なるほどくん！”
English: "Hi, I'm Nick!"

Japanese: 声が小さいのかな。
English: Maybe I'm not saying it loud enough.

Japanese: “なーるーほーどーくーん！！”
English: "HEY! I'M NICK!!!"

Japanese: オカシイな‥‥。
English: Huh.

Japanese: なあーるうー ほおーどおー‥‥
English: NIIIIIIIIII... ...IIIIIIIIIICK!

Japanese: や、やめてくれ ハズかしい！
English: Will you stop that?

Japanese: コワれてるよ、このキカイ！
English: Maybe it's broken?

Japanese: けるな！
English: D-don't kick it!

Japanese: ‥‥人間の声には 反応しないんじゃないか？
English: Maybe it isn't set to respond to voices?

Japanese: じゃあ、なに？ これなら反応するの？
English: Well what then? I know!

Japanese: クラッカー？
English: The party popper...?

Japanese: パァァァン！
English: *BANG*

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik

Japanese: カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！ カシャ　カシャ　カシャ　カシャ！
English: klik klik klik klik klik klik klik klik

Japanese: ‥‥反応したよ‥‥。
English: Yep. It responded.

Japanese: 音に反応して、自動的に シャッターが切られるカメラ。
English: This camera is rigged to respond to noise.

Japanese: クラッカーなどの、ハレツ音に セットされているようだ。
English: But it only responds to loud noises... like a party popper.

Japanese: ‥‥あのう。
English: Umm...

Japanese: なんや！
English: What!?

Japanese: ウチは今、カメラのフィルムを かえとるんや！
English: Can't ya see I'm changin' the film on my camera here!

Japanese: どこかのアホが、１本まるまる ムダにしてくれたし！
English: Someone--I'm not namin' any names--but someone used up a WHOLE ROLL.

Japanese: ゴメンナサイ‥‥。
English: Sorry...

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ）
English: (That didn't work...)

Japanese: （何か、気を引けそうなものを 見せた方がいいな‥‥）
English: (I wonder if I have anything to show her that would get her attention...)

Japanese: なあなあ。サツジン事件て、 いつやったん？
English: Oh yeah, when was that murder, anyway?

Japanese: ウチ、ここんとこずっと テレビ見てへんねん。
English: I ain't seen much television lately.

Japanese: クリスマスイブの深夜です。
English: It happened late in the night on Christmas Eve.

Japanese: ふうん。 くりすますいぶ、ねえ‥‥。
English: That so? Christmas Eve?

Japanese: 男の人がボートの上で 撃たれたんですけど‥‥、
English: A man on a boat was shot...

Japanese: 何か見ませんでした？
English: Did you see anything?

Japanese: ううーん。
English: Well, lemme see.

Japanese: ‥‥ボートなあ。
English: A boat, ya say?

Japanese: 見たような気もするねんけど‥‥。 ハッキリせえへんなあ。
English: I reckon I mighta seen one... not sure though.

Japanese: ここ３日間、ずーっと湖を 見てるやろ？
English: Y'all gotta remember I've been watching this here lake for a good three days now.

Japanese: ボートもクサるほど見とるワケや。 いつのコトかは、おぼえてへん。
English: I seen enough boats to choke a mule. Kinda hard to remember which I seen when.

Japanese: なんか、すごいカメラですね？
English: That's quite a camera you have there.

Japanese: そうやろ？ ドイツ製やで。ゾリンゲン。
English: Y'all better know it! It's German-made. A genuine Solingen!

Japanese: （そりゃ刃物だろ‥‥）
English: (Isn't that where they make knives...?)

Japanese: あの‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: カメラについてる 装置は、なんですか？
English: So, what's that device you have stuck to the camera?

Japanese: あ、ああ。あれ‥‥？
English: Huh? Device?

Japanese: クラッカー鳴らしたら、 急に動き出しちゃって‥‥。
English: It started moving all by itself when I fired my party popper...

Japanese: 大きな音がしたら、自動的に シャッターを切るから。
English: Oh that? That triggers the shutter whenever it detects certain sounds.

Japanese: ハレツするような音が、 装置にプログラムされてるんや。
English: It's programmed to pick up loud noises right now.

Japanese: へええ‥‥。 ぷろぐらむされてるんですねえ。
English: A programmable camera! Neat!

Japanese: 《ナツミのカメラ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Lotta's Camera added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なんか、すごいカメラですね？
English: That's quite a camera you have there.

Japanese: そうやろ？ ドイツ製やで。ゾリンゲン。
English: Y'all better know it! It's German-made. A genuine Solingen!

Japanese: （そりゃ刃物だろ‥‥）
English: (Isn't that where they make knives...?)

Japanese: あの‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: カメラについてる 装置は、なんですか？
English: So, what's that device you have stuck to the camera?

Japanese: あ、ああ。あれ‥‥？
English: Huh? Device?

Japanese: クラッカー鳴らしたら、 急に動き出しちゃって‥‥。
English: It started moving all by itself when I fired my party popper...

Japanese: 大きな音がしたら、自動的に シャッターを切るから。
English: Oh that? That triggers the shutter whenever it detects certain sounds.

Japanese: ハレツするような音が 装置にプログラムされてるんや。
English: It's programmed to pick up loud noises right now.

Japanese: へええ‥‥。 ぷろぐらむされてるんですねえ。
English: A programmable camera! Neat!

Japanese: ナツミさんて、 どういう方なんですか？
English: So, what is it you do, Lotta?

Japanese: え、ウチ？
English: Huh? Me?

Japanese: は、はは。 どうだってええやん、そんなコト。
English: Hah hah. Y'all don't really want to know that, do ya?

Japanese: 大阪のな、大学生や。 ‥‥研究員やってるんや。
English: Actually, I'm a research student at Country U., right in the heart of the heartland.

Japanese: うわあ、スゴい！
English: Wow! Neat!

Japanese: なるほどくん！　大阪で、大学で 研究員だよ！
English: Nick! She's a research student at a university! Country U.!

Japanese: ‥‥そのまんまだな。
English: Uh... so I hear.

Japanese: それで、いつから ここにいるんですか？
English: So, when did you come up here?

Japanese: そ、そうやなあ‥‥。 ３日間ぐらい、やろか‥‥。
English: Hmm, lemme see... I guess it was 'bout three days ago.

Japanese: 何を撮影してるんですか？
English: What are you photographing?

Japanese: さ、さっきも言ったやろ？
English: D-didn't I tell y'all that already?

Japanese: 流星や。そう、流星群を 撮っとるんや。
English: Meteors! Yep, meteor showers!
(Falling stars...?)

Japanese: あの。ぼく、こういうモノ なんですけど‥‥。
English: I, er, this is my badge.

Japanese: どういうモノや？　そんな こきたないバッジ、知らん。
English: Huh? Aren't badges supposed t' be all shiny and impressive? You a cop or something?

Japanese: 弁護士なんですけど‥‥。
English: Umm... I'm a lawyer.

Japanese: な、なんや！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: さっきのフィルムのことで、 インネンつける気ィなん？
English: Y'all ain't gonna try and pull one o' them lawsuits on me over that film now?

Japanese: ええで。 ‥‥出るトコ出よか！
English: Cause I'll have y'all know I'm a fighter and I wrassled meaner lookin' things'n you!

Japanese: い、いえいえそうじゃなくて。
English: N-no, that's not it at all.

Japanese: ぼくたち、この湖で起こった 殺人事件を調べてるんですよ。
English: We're here investigating a murder that took place here, on the lake.

Japanese: サツジンジケン‥‥？
English: A murder...?

Japanese: なんや、オモロそうやん！
English: Sounds cool!

Japanese: ‥‥うん。ええわ。 なんでも聞きィ。
English: Why didn't y'all say that in the first place! Go ahead, ask me anything ya like!

Japanese: （おお。やった！ キゲンがなおったみたいだ）
English: (Finally! Some cooperation!)

Japanese: アンタも、かくれてないで 出て来ィ。
English: You too. Y'all can come out of hiding now. I won't bite. Hard.

Japanese: （そういえば、真宵ちゃんの すがたが‥‥）
English: (Come to think of it, where did Maya get to?)

Japanese: ゴメンナサイ‥‥
English: S-sorry...

Japanese: あたし、早くしゃべるの ニガテで‥‥。
English: I-I was feeling a little overwhelmed. The culture gap and all...

Japanese: ええてええて。 ウチが合わせたる。
English: Never you mind, honey. I kin' talk Yank for ya if... ahem... if it pleases you?

Japanese: は、はい！　‥‥ありがとう！
English: Th-thanks. I think I'll be okay.

Japanese: ウチは大沢木　ナツミ （おおさわぎなつみ）や。
English: Great then! I'm Lotta, Lotta Hart, but y'all can call me Lotta!

Japanese: 大学の研究生で、 流星の撮影に来てるんや。
English: I'm here photographing meteor showers for a research project.

Japanese: ヨロシクな。
English: Mighty pleased ta meet ya!

Japanese: それにしても、 アンタが弁護士ねぇ‥‥。
English: So yer a lawyer, huh?

Japanese: ハッキリ言って、 バッジ負けしてるなァ。あははは。
English: Tell the truth, yer badge is a lot more impressive than you are.

Japanese: あははは。
English: Hah hah!

Japanese: キッ！
English: *glare*

Japanese: うううう。
English: Hah... hah... oh. Ahem.

Japanese: ナツミさん。
English: Lotta?

Japanese: ん？
English: Yeah?

Japanese: “ハレツ音”で作動するんですね？ ‥‥あのカメラ。
English: So, your camera... it triggers on loud explosion noises?

Japanese: そうや。
English: Ayup.

Japanese: この事件の被害者、ピストルで 撃たれてるんですよ。
English: Actually, the victim in the case we're researching--he was shot with a pistol.

Japanese: ぴすとる‥‥？
English: A pistol...?

Japanese: 銃声って、クラッカーと同じ “ハレツ音”ですよね？
English: Right. Now wouldn't a gunshot make a similar noise to our party popper...?

Japanese: ‥‥そうやね。
English: I guess it would.

Japanese: もしかして、何か撮影されて いないでしょうか‥‥？
English: Your camera... didn't get a picture of the murder, did it?

Japanese: おお！
English: Hey!

Japanese: なんか、カッコエエなあ ニイちゃん。
English: Y'all are pretty bright!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥うんうん、なるほどなあ。
English: I see what yer sayin'...

Japanese: ちょっとウチ、調べてみたるわ。
English: Tell ya what, I'll have a look-see at my film.

Japanese: 昨日の晩の写真なんですけど。
English: It would have been a photo taken late last night...

Japanese: チェックはしたんやけど、 何か写っとるかもしれんもんな。
English: I checked 'em once, don't remember if there was anything on 'em though.

Japanese: へへ。ちょっとワクワク するなあ。
English: But what if I got sumtin'! I could be witness to a genuine murder! Yeehaw!

Japanese: ほな、またあとで来ィ。
English: I'll go check that film. Y'all come back now, y'hear?

Japanese: クルマの中に入っちゃったよ。
English: She went inside her SUV.

Japanese: しょうがないね。 ‥‥またあとで来ようか。
English: I guess we should come back later...

Japanese: なんやそれ。
English: What y'all got there? Say, deep fry that and it'd make some fine hush puppies!

Japanese: （‥‥関心がないものには ものすごくタンパクだな）
English: (I'd better hang on to any evidence I want to keep...)

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 ひょうたん森
English: December 25 Gourd Lake Woods

Japanese: あっ！　アンタら！
English: Hey y'all!

Japanese: ナツミさん！
English: Lotta!

Japanese: 待っとったんやで！ ‥‥ビンゴやビンゴ！
English: Wait up a sec! We got bingo!

Japanese: ‥‥びんご？
English: Bingo...?

Japanese: 昨日の晩なぁ、２回、 自動シャッターが下りてん。
English: My automatic camera took two pictures last night!

Japanese: うんうん。
English: Hey!

Japanese: その２発目が、これや！ ちょっと、見てみィや！
English: This is them. Take a look!

Japanese: これは‥‥！
English: Wait...!

Japanese: な？　な？　思いっきり 撃っとるやろ？　ピストル。
English: See? See? He's shooting him with that pistol!

Japanese: え、ええ。
English: I-it looks like that, yes.

Japanese: でも、誰が撃ったかまでは 見えませんねー。
English: But you can't really tell who that is shooting.

Japanese: そーやねえ。 ‥‥キリが濃かったからなァ。
English: Yeah, well there was enough fog out there last night t' strangle a bullfrog.

Japanese: でもな。
English: But, y'know...

Japanese: この写真見て、ウチ、 思い出したんや。
English: Seeing these photos reminded me of something.

Japanese: な‥‥何をですか？
English: What...?

Japanese: この事件なァ、ウチ、 目撃しててん。
English: I saw the murder happen. I'm a witness!

Japanese: ええええええええええっ！
English: Whaaaaaaa!?

Japanese: ほ、ほんとですかナツミさん！
English: A-are you serious!?

Japanese: モチや。
English: Course!

Japanese: （ま‥‥また、トンデモナイ コトに‥‥）
English: (How do you forget... never mind.)

Japanese: なあなあなあ、これってヤッパ、 ケーサツにゆうた方がええよなァ？
English: Y'all reckon I should tell the cops?

Japanese: ‥‥ゆうた方が ええんちゃいます？
English: I... reckon so!

Japanese: アンタ！
English: What's that?

Japanese: インチキな関西弁、 使ォたらアカンで！
English: Now don't y'all go tryin' to mock my accent!

Japanese: ‥‥まァ、ええワ。
English: I'm a sensitive lady!

Japanese: ‥‥やめといた方が ええんちゃいます？
English: I... reckon no!

Japanese: アンタ！
English: What's that?

Japanese: インチキな関西弁で ゴマかそうゆうつもりやな！
English: Now don't y'all think y'all can pull one over on me just cause you speak the lingo!

Japanese: サツジンを見たら、ケーサツに タレこむ。
English: I know enough to know that when you see a murder, you'd best tell the cops!

Japanese: これは、市民のギムや。
English: It's my obligation as a citizen!

Japanese: ‥‥ほな、ウチ、ちょっと ケーサツ行ってくるわ。
English: Hey, so, I'm off to talk to the cops.

Japanese: この写真、やるから。
English: Y'all can have this photo.

Japanese: ほな。
English: Later.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょ、ちょっと！ ナツミさん！
English: W-w-wait! Lotta!

Japanese: なんや？ ‥‥忙しいんや、ウチは。
English: What? Can't y'all see I'm kinda busy?

Japanese: ぼくたちにも、その話、 聞かせてくださいよ！
English: T-tell us what you saw, too! Please?

Japanese: アホ。
English: Nice try, honey, but I wasn't born yesterday.

Japanese: ウチ、目撃者やで。 セイギの味方、ケーサツの味方や。
English: I'm a witness, and that means I'm on the side of justice, and that means the cops!

Japanese: アンタらに教えるわけ、 ないやん。
English: I'd sooner eat the south side of a north-bound skunk than tell you!

Japanese: ひどい、ナツミさん‥‥。
English: L-Lotta!

Japanese: まま、ええやん。 昨日のテキは今日の友。逆か。
English: Don't let it get your skivvies in a bunch. Friends today, enemies tomorrow!

Japanese: とにかく、そーゆーことや。 ‥‥ほな、行くで。
English: Or was that the other way round? No matter. I'm gone!

Japanese: へへ。証言とかするんかなァ。 ‥‥楽しそうやなー。
English: Hey! Maybe they'll let me do some testifying! Hot darn!

Japanese: 行っちゃった‥‥。
English: She left...

Japanese: どうしよう。 目撃者、ふやしちゃったよ‥‥。
English: Well, that's one more witness. What do we do now, Nick?

Japanese: （‥‥でも、見ちゃったものは しょうがないもんなあ‥‥）
English: (Well, if she saw something, there's not much we can do about it.)

Japanese: （問題は、“ナツミさんが 何を見たか”だけど‥‥）
English: (The question is: what exactly did she see...?)

Japanese: （それはどうやら、裁判まで 知りようがないな‥‥）
English: (I guess we'll find out in the trial tomorrow.)

Japanese: 証拠品《湖の写真》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Lake Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 25 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: イトノコさん、いないね。
English: Looks like Detective Gumshoe isn't here.

Japanese: どうしたの？　おじょうちゃん？
English: Something wrong, miss?

Japanese: ん？　自首かな。
English: Hmm? Turning yourself in?

Japanese: 何やったの？　まんびき？
English: Okay, what did you do? Shoplifting? Larceny? Public indecency...?

Japanese: ち、ちち違いますよ！
English: N-no! None of those things!

Japanese: イトノコ刑事さん、 探してるんですけど‥‥。
English: We're looking for Detective Gumshoe... is he around?

Japanese: イトノコ‥‥？
English: Gumshoe?

Japanese: ああ。 今、捜査会議やってるよ。
English: Oh yeah. He's in a meeting right now.

Japanese: まだ、かかるんじゃないかなあ。
English: I don't think he'll be out any time soon.

Japanese: じゃあ、また来ます。
English: Okay, we'll come back.

Japanese: そうしなさい。
English: You do that.

Japanese: まっすぐ帰るんだよ。
English: Oh, and go straight home and stay out of trouble.

Japanese: まんびきなんかしちゃ ダメだからね。
English: No more shoplifting, you got that?

Japanese: あたし、悪人ヅラなのかな‥‥。
English: Do I look like a criminal or something?

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 25 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: イトノコさん、 まだカイギなのかな‥‥。
English: I guess Detective Gumshoe is still in that meeting.

Japanese: おおッ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: よく来たッス！
English: Thanks for coming down, pal!

Japanese: あっ！　イトノコさん！
English: Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: 捜査会議が終わったッス。 ‥‥やれやれッス。
English: We just finished the meeting. For better or for worse...

Japanese: （あまり、いい知らせは なさそうだな‥‥）
English: (I get the feeling we're in for some bad news...)

Japanese: 被害者のこと、 何かわかりましたか？
English: Do you know anything about the victim yet?

Japanese: それが‥‥。 まだ、身元がわからないッス。
English: No, no... Still can't I.D. him.

Japanese: 御剣検事は、なんて‥‥？
English: Has Mr. Edgeworth said anything?

Japanese: 何も言ってくれないッス‥‥。
English: Not a word.

Japanese: どうなりましたか？ ‥‥捜査会議。
English: So, how did the meeting go?

Japanese: そんなこと、弁護士になんか 話せるワケ、ないッス！
English: I can't tell you, pal! You're a lawyer!

Japanese: そ、そうですよね‥‥。
English: T-true...
Y'know...

Japanese: 自分はもう、何を 信じていいかわからねッス。
English: I don't know what to believe anymore.

Japanese: たしかに、御剣検事も ワレワレと同じ、ニンゲンッス。
English: Sure, Mr. Edgeworth's human like you or me.

Japanese: でも。
English: Still...

Japanese: もしあの人がツミを犯したら、 かくさないと思うッス。
English: I get the feeling that if he'd done something wrong, he wouldn't go hiding it.

Japanese: あの人は、そういう人ッス。
English: That's just the kind of guy he is.

Japanese: なんでみんな、それが わからないッスか！
English: Why can't anyone else see that?

Japanese: やっぱり、御剣検事が 犯人ということで‥‥？
English: So, they think that Mr. Edgeworth did it...?

Japanese: 明日の裁判は、予定どおり やるッス。
English: Well, the trial's starting tomorrow, as scheduled.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: ‥‥捜査会議のこと、結局 ゼンブしゃべっちゃいましたね。
English: Umm... hey, in the end you did tell us about the meeting!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。 みんなにはヒミツッス。

English: Don't go telling anyone else, pal.

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-yes, sir!

Japanese: とにかく、おねがいするッス！
English: And... do me a favor!

Japanese: どうか、御剣検事を見捨てないで ほしいッス！
English: Stand by Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: あの人には今、味方が必要ッス！
English: He needs help, and you're the ones to help him!

Japanese: 何も話してくれないのは、 きっと理由があるハズッス！
English: I'm sure he's got some reason why he won't talk to us.

Japanese: イトノコさん‥‥。
English: Thanks, Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: イトノコさん‥‥
English: Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: なぜ、そこまで御剣検事を 信じられるんですか？
English: How come you trust Mr. Edgeworth so much?

Japanese: そんなこと、トーゼンッス。
English: Well, I'd think that was obvious.

Japanese: ワレワレには、カターい 信頼関係があるッス。
English: We got a strong working relationship, us two.

Japanese: おたがいを信じているから、 いっしょにがんばれるッス。
English: We trust each other, and that's how it works.

Japanese: “信頼関係”‥‥？
English: A "working relationship"?

Japanese: 御剣検事は、起訴された被告人を 必ず有罪にするッス。
English: See, Mr. Edgeworth always gets his defendants declared "guilty" every time.

Japanese: たしかに、行きすぎた 方法を使うときもあるッス。
English: Yeah, his methods might be a little extreme at times.

Japanese: でも、それは‥‥
English: But... there's a reason!

Japanese: ワレワレの捜査を、信頼して くれているからこそッス。
English: He trusts our investigation, see? He trusts us to get the right man!

Japanese: そして、その信頼に こたえるために、
English: That's why I work extra hard, pal.

Japanese: ワレワレは、必死で捜査を しなければならないッス。
English: We've got to earn that trust he places in us.

Japanese: ‥‥そうなんですか。
English: I see...

Japanese: 御剣検事は、信頼できる 人物ッス！
English: Mr. Edgeworth is a man you can trust...

Japanese: それだけは、この糸鋸　圭介、 誓って言えるッス！
English: And you have my word on that!

Japanese: そういえば‥‥。 解剖記録、どうなりました？
English: I was wondering, did you ever get that autopsy report?

Japanese: ああ。それッス。 コピーしといたッス。
English: Oh, that? Yeah, I made a copy for you.

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Added Autopsy Report to the Court Record.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。なるほどくん？
English: ...Nick?

Japanese: ん？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ちょっと見せて‥‥ その、被害者の写真。
English: Can you show me that photo of the victim?

Japanese: ‥‥どこかで‥‥。
English: That face...!

Japanese: 知ってる人？
English: Someone you know?

Japanese: ‥‥ううん、わからない。
English: I... I don't know.

Japanese: ムカシどこかで‥‥ 会ったことがあるような‥‥。
English: I just have this feeling that I met him somewhere a long time ago.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、 考え込んでいる‥‥）
English: (Maya seems lost in thought.)

Japanese: どう？　何か思いだした？
English: You remember anything?

Japanese: （ダメみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Guess not.)

Japanese: 何か役に立てば、なによりッス。
English: Well, I hope it helps you, pal.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thanks, Detective.

Japanese: ‥‥ベツに、思い当たるコト、 ないッスね‥‥。
English: Sorry, I'm drawing a blank right now.

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 25 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: またいないよ、イトノコさん。
English: Not a Gumshoe in sight.

Japanese: イトノコなら、取り調べ室だよ。
English: If you're looking for Detective Gumshoe, he's in the questioning room.

Japanese: ちょっと、重要な証人が あらわれたんでね。
English: Apparently, an important witness turned up.

Japanese: 時間、かかると思うよ。
English: He'll be in there a while.

Japanese: ナツミさんだよ‥‥。
English: Lotta Hart... it has to be.

Japanese: まいったなあ‥‥。
English: Uh oh...

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 25 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: どういうことッスか！
English: What's going on here!?

Japanese: きゃっ！
English: Eek!

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか？
English: W-what's wrong, Detective?

Japanese: さっき、ヘンな女が たずねて来たッス！
English: This wild lady comes in here just a while ago...

Japanese: “なるほどーさんの しょうかいで来ましてん”とか
English: Says she came "to talk to y'all after hearing what Mr. Wright had to say"!

Japanese: 言ってたッス！
English: What's this all about, pal!?

Japanese: （‥‥ナツミさんか‥‥）
English: (Lotta Hart...)

Japanese: 目撃者、ふやして どーするッスか！
English: Why are you going around finding more witnesses!?

Japanese: 御剣検事を死刑にする気ッスか！
English: You want to give Mr. Edgeworth the death sentence, pal!?

Japanese: い、いや、でも‥‥。
English: N-no, not at all...

Japanese: 見ちゃったモノは しょうがないですよ。
English: Just... I mean, she did see something. There's nothing I can do about that.

Japanese: かくすワケにもいかないでしょう？
English: I can't go around covering up evidence!

Japanese: ううううう。
English: Er...

Japanese: ‥‥それは、キベンッス！
English: You trying to say something about the way I do my job?

Japanese: いやいやいや。
English: No sir!

Japanese: それで‥‥ナツミさんは、なんて？
English: So... what did Ms. Hart say?

Japanese: 御剣検事がピストルを撃つところを 見ていたそうッス。
English: She says she saw Mr. Edgeworth fire the pistol.

Japanese: えええええッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: しかも、証拠写真まであったッス。
English: She even had a photograph to prove it.

Japanese: ああ。 ‥‥それなら、ぼくも見ましたよ。
English: Right. I saw it too...

Japanese: この写真じゃあ、誰が撃ったか 見えませんけどね‥‥。
English: But you really can't tell from the photo who it is shooting.

Japanese: 彼女、これからその写真を 拡大するって言ってたッス。
English: That's why she said she's going to enlarge the photo.

Japanese: 画像はアラくなるけど、もう少し ハッキリするはずや！　‥‥とか。
English: She said "it'll drop the quality a mite, but should let us see who's who!"

Japanese: カ、カクダイ‥‥。
English: She can do that!?

Japanese: （間違いなく、御剣に不利な証拠 として、拡大写真が出てくるぞ）
English: (Okay, so there's going to be an enlarged photograph that shows Edgeworth in the act.)

Japanese: （‥‥マズいなあ）
English: (Great. Just great.)

Japanese: とにかく、明日の証人は 彼女ッス。
English: In any case, she's going to be the one testifying tomorrow.

Japanese: あれ？
English: Huh?

Japanese: もう１人の目撃者は どうしたんですか？
English: What happened to the other witness?

Japanese: それが‥‥。
English: Well...

Japanese: とりあえず、キャンセルに なったみたいッス。
English: Apparently, there was a cancellation.

Japanese: （“キャンセル”‥‥？）
English: (A "cancellation"...!?)

Japanese: 明日は、御剣検事にとっては、 いきなり絶体絶命ッス。
English: I'm afraid tomorrow is going to be life or death for poor Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 目撃者は、ハッキリ犯行の シュンカンを見ていると言うし、
English: We got a witness who says she saw the very moment of the murder.

Japanese: 発砲したときの写真まで 出てくるッス。
English: And we got a photo taken when the shot rang out.

Japanese: （‥‥どう考えても、 勝てそうもないぞ‥‥）
English: (I'd say that sounds like a pretty unwinnable case...)

Japanese: （でも‥‥。 千尋さんが教えてくれた）
English: (But wait... What did Mia used to say?)

Japanese: （アイツが無罪なら、どこかに 見落としがあるんだ‥‥）
English: (If he's innocent, there's got to be something I've overlooked...)

Japanese: 御剣検事は、国選弁護人を たのむつもりらしいッス。
English: It sounds like Mr. Edgeworth is going to ask the state to assign a public defender.

Japanese: さっき、手続きをたのまれたッス。
English: I was just asked to file the paperwork.

Japanese: でも、まだ間に合うッス！
English: But you still got time, pal!

Japanese: もう一度、検事に会って ほしいッス！
English: Go talk to him again, for me, please!

Japanese: そして‥‥アンタが！
English: You have to convince him!

Japanese: アンタが、彼の弁護をするッス！ おねがいッス！
English: You have to make him let you defend him! Please!

Japanese: 彼を助けられるのは、 アンタしかいないッス！
English: I know you're the only one who can do it, pal.
You're the only one who can save Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: もらってきましたよ。これ。
English: Look what I got.

Japanese: それッス！
English: Hey, you did it, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥ギリギリまで待った カイがあったッス！
English: Glad I waited till the last minute to file those papers!

Japanese: じゃあ、さっそく手続きを しておくッス！
English: I'll rip 'em up and start new ones for you!

Japanese: おねがいします。
English: Thanks, Detective.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、明日、法廷で。
English: Well, see you in court tomorrow, then.

Japanese: よろしく、たのむッス。
English: Good luck, pal.

Japanese: おっ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: そういえば、さっき地震が あったッスが、
English: You guys feel that earthquake a little while back?

Japanese: だいじょうぶだったッスか？
English: I was worried!

Japanese: やだなあ。
English: Worried?

Japanese: 地震ぐらい、どうってこと ないですよお。
English: We're fine! I've lived out here my whole life. I'm pretty used to them by now.

Japanese: アンタたちじゃないッス。
English: Oh, I wasn't worried about you two.

Japanese: 御剣検事は、だいじょうぶ だったッスか？
English: I was worried about Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: （‥‥そういえば‥‥）
English: (Oh, right...)

Japanese: なんか、オーバーに こわがっていましたけど‥‥。
English: He did seem to over-react a little, now that you mention it.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。さっきの地震は ちょっと大きかったッスからね。
English: Yeah, well, I'm not surprised. It was a pretty big quake.

Japanese: ちょっと、見てくるッス。
English: I'm going to go check on him.

Japanese: アンタたちは、明日にそなえて メシ食って寝るッス。
English: You two go eat and get your rest for tomorrow's trial.

Japanese: じゃッ！
English: Later!

Japanese: どういうこと？　なるほどくん？ 御剣検事、地震がニガテなの？
English: I wonder what it is with Mr. Edgeworth and earthquakes?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。
English: I wonder...

Japanese: そんなの、聞いたことないぞ。
English: He was never that scared of them when he was in school.

Japanese: （ぼくたちがいっしょだったのは、 小学４年生の１年間）
English: (Then again, I was only in the same class as him for 4th grade.)

Japanese: （その後、御剣は転校していった）
English: (He transferred to another school after that.)

Japanese: （‥‥いったい、やつの身に 何があったんだろうか‥‥）
English: (I wonder what happened to Edgeworth...)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: アンタに見せてもらいたいのは、 ただ１つ！
English: Hey, pal, there's only one thing I want to see from you!

Japanese: 御剣検事からの依頼状ッス！
English: And that's a letter of request from Mr. Edgeworth for you to be his defense attorney!

Japanese: １２月２５日　某時刻 星影法律事務所
English: December 25 Grossberg Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥ひさしぶりだなあ。 センセイの事務所。
English: It's been a while since I was here last.

Japanese: あいかわらず、 お出かけ中らしいな。
English: Mr. Grossberg is out as usual.

Japanese: ねえねえ、なるほどくん。 ‥‥あそこのカベ。
English: Hey, Nick. Look at the wall.

Japanese: （あの絵‥‥。 かえってこないんだ‥‥）
English: (That painting is still missing...)

Japanese: （千尋さん、星影センセイが 小中に脅迫されていたこと）
English: (Mia must have known about the deal with Mr. Grossberg and Redd White...)

Japanese: （もちろん、 知っていたんだろうな‥‥）
English: (She kept track of all of White's extortion and blackmail rackets.)

Japanese: あそこのカベ、なんで 色が違うんだろ？
English: Why is the wall there a different color?

Japanese: この前まで、大きな絵が かかっていたんだよ。
English: A big painting was hanging there until recently.

Japanese: ふうん。 ‥‥どうしたの、その絵？
English: Huh. What happened to it?

Japanese: ううん‥‥ヒトに あげちゃったんだ。
English: Uh... well, he gave it to someone.

Japanese: ‥‥わあ。 “オイラクの恋”ってヤツだね。
English: I bet he gave it to some romantic interest! Love blooms eternal, Nick!

Japanese: ま、そんなトコかな。
English: Uh... yeah.

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりした デスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまで みがき込まれている。
English: The wood's been polished to a deep luster.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 あたしも、あんな机がいいなあ。
English: Hey, Nick! I want a desk like that in our office too!

Japanese: まだまだ、ぼくには にあわないと思うよ。
English: I don't know. I don't think I'm ready to sit at a desk like that yet.
Huh?

Japanese: あたしが使うんだよ？
English: I meant for me!

Japanese: ‥‥おこづかいためて買えば。
English: You'd better start saving your allowance, then.

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、 純金製のライター、かな？
